c1: Gravel is compiled to LLVM, this ensures a clean transition, avoiding human errors at translating to assembly. Also, LLVM includes a optimizer, used for making execution way faster.
c2: Gravel compiler evaluates as much as possible during compilation, reducing CPU and RAM usage at execution, while making the experience smoother.
c3: Gravel has no secrets. No strange convertions, no surprises. Types never change. They are explicit. But you can use inference for native types (int, char, float).
c4: Gravel is simple and predictable. Errors and warnings help. Reads as pseudocode or high level.
c5: Gravel is low level and lets you control the hardware, but its syntax is clean and logical, making writing it enjoyable.
